env.sglang: v0.5.12 + RDNA4 patches (incl. 041 AWQ GEMV, 042 FP8 reclaim)

infra_fix: iommu=pt cleared the post-131K-prefill TP2 NCCL channel-renegotiation wedge: NCCL log lines 178278 -> 4 (events ceased, not just logging), no-spec 131K decode 0.68 -> 16.83 tok/s. Short-context P2P never tripped it (why the 97 tok/s short-ctx runs missed it).
method: single-user decode tok/s = 1000 / Mean_TPOT_ms via sglang.bench_serving (random dataset); spec-decode also measured on coherent coding prompts. context label = bench random-input length (repo convention input=ctx/2).

quality.basic: PASS (coherent code under no-spec and spec)

Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B-FP8.notes: no-spec long-context curve + EAGLE3 coherent ~86 tok/s are post-iommu=pt real data; pilot pre-fix no-spec 256K (0.68) and spec (2.56/72) DISCARDED. accept_len 5.5 confirms topk16 tree active (chain would be ~2-3); the pilot's 'tree kernel unavailable/greedy' message did NOT recur. 256K random spec 2.44 is a no-wedge sanity check only (random tokens => ~0 draft acceptance), not coherent throughput.

validate.raw: 3/3; basic='paris', thinking 446tok finish=stop, vision saw red/circle/round

spec.ctx_ceiling: OOM at 262K prefill on 2x32GB — BF16 draft + DFlash verify + DeltaNet/fused-MoE prefill activations exceed VRAM at every mem-fraction that also yields a usable KV pool (0.85->17-tok KV; 0.88->110K KV short-only; 0.92->OOM gdn_backend conv1d @131K; 0.80->startup OOM)

Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8.notes: FP8 no-spec is the 256K-capable path (1.6M-tok KV, 17-18 tok/s, 3/3 caps). DFlash gives strong SHORT-context speedup (57.5 tok/s, 3.2x, accept 3.0, tree active) but is NOT 256K-capable in FP8 (draft+verify+hybrid-prefill OOM). For 256K+spec use the AWQ-int4 target (half the weight bytes -> headroom); the README/specdecode.json '80 tok/s @ 256K' was the AWQ ship, NOT FP8 — correct those.

spec.note: no spec path (MistralForCausalLMEagle would load a SpecForge-trained draft; none exists for Devstral)

Devstral-Small-2-24B-FP8.notes: At devstral2 defaults (mem 0.90, fp8 KV) KV pool tops out at max_total=180572 < 262144 — a single sequence CANNOT reach full 256K; largest validated IN=131072. CONTRADICTS the devstral2 preset comment ('patch-042 reclaim -> 413K -> full 256K') — that figure was likely the v1 dense Devstral-24B-FP8 (no vision tower) or a pre-vision build; v2 carries a BF16 vision tower that eats the KV budget. FOLLOW-UP (#10): retest at mem 0.92 to see if 256K single-seq is reachable (documented +22% lever). basic+vision+tool all PASS; no draft.
